message:
feat(net/tls): add trust-root source config surface and fix io_adapter read extensions

Expose `asx_tls_root_source` enum and `asx_tls_config_set_root_source()` in the
public TLS header so callers can explicitly select native-roots vs webpki-roots
(or receive ASX_E_PERMISSION_DENIED for unsupported variants on browser builds).
This closes the last implicit-contract gap in the TLS trust-root feature matrix.

Also fixes io_adapter read extension implementations and adds corresponding unit
tests for the TLS config surface and bytes io_adapter.

- include/asx/net/tls.h: add asx_tls_root_source enum + setter
- src/net/tls.c: implement root source configuration with permission checks
- src/bytes/io_adapter.c: fix read extension implementations
- tests/unit/bytes/test_io_adapter.c: add read extension tests
- tests/unit/net/test_tls.c: add TLS root source config tests
- docs/PROFILE_RESOURCE_CLASS_CAPABILITY_MATRIX.md: update TLS row
- .beads/issues.jsonl: close bd-nchf TLS trust-root task

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Reinstall hooks after reset in the affected lifecycle tests and restore the red unit lane.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-16T12:54:27.359560380Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-16T12:56:25.415818832Z","closed_at":"2026-03-16T12:56:25.415167669Z","close_reason":"Fixed failing fault injection lifecycle tests by reinstalling hooks after runtime_reset clears hook state","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bug","runtime","tests"]}

@@ -273,7 +273,7 @@ The table below maps the crate-root feature/platform restrictions from
 | `browser-trace` forbidden with upstream minimal browser profile | Browser minimal-vs-extended subprofiles now gate the public trace family | `partial` | `include/asx/asx_config.h` now exposes `ASX_HAS_BROWSER_TRACE == 0` under `ASX_BROWSER_PROFILE_MINIMAL`, `include/asx/asx.h` omits `trace`/`telemetry`/`replay`/`lab`/`regression_localize`/`tracing_compat` from the umbrella in minimal browser builds, and the direct headers now self-gate those families unless internal implementation files opt in via `ASX_INTERNAL_TRACE_FAMILY_ACCESS`; remaining debt is broader upstream feature parity beyond this trace-family split |
 | `cli` unsupported on browser builds | Public CLI family exists and is now browser-hidden | `partial` | The C tree ships `include/asx/cli/cli.h` and `src/cli/cli.c`, and browser builds now hide that family both from `include/asx/asx.h` and from direct `include/asx/cli/cli.h` inclusion via `ASX_HAS_NATIVE_RUNTIME_SURFACES`; remaining debt is broader compile-time/browser feature-matrix coverage beyond the CLI surface itself |
 | `io-uring` unsupported on browser builds | No io_uring feature surface in C | `mapped-by-absence` | No equivalent feature exists yet; once native reactor features expand, browser incompatibility must become explicit |
-| `tls`, `tls-native-roots`, `tls-webpki-roots` unsupported on browser builds | Browser boundary denies the shipped TLS family in browser mode and the trust-root variants are now explicit compile-time absences | `partial` | `ASX_SURFACE_TLS` is fail-closed at the boundary and in the status-returning TLS entry points, `include/asx/asx_config.h` exposes `ASX_HAS_TLS_SURFACE == 0` under browser builds, and the trust-root variants are now represented explicitly via `ASX_HAS_TLS_NATIVE_ROOTS == 0`, `ASX_HAS_TLS_WEBPKI_ROOTS == 0`, and `ASX_HAS_TLS_ROOT_VARIANTS == 0`; remaining debt is actual implementation breadth rather than undocumented absence |
+| `tls`, `tls-native-roots`, `tls-webpki-roots` unsupported on browser builds | Browser boundary denies the shipped TLS family in browser mode and the trust-root variants are now modeled in the public config surface as unsupported selections | `partial` | `ASX_SURFACE_TLS` is fail-closed at the boundary and in the status-returning TLS entry points, `include/asx/asx_config.h` exposes `ASX_HAS_TLS_SURFACE == 0` under browser builds, `include/asx/net/tls.h` now exposes `asx_tls_root_source` plus `asx_tls_config_set_root_source()`, and the trust-root variants remain explicit absences via `ASX_HAS_TLS_NATIVE_ROOTS == 0`, `ASX_HAS_TLS_WEBPKI_ROOTS == 0`, and `ASX_HAS_TLS_ROOT_VARIANTS == 0`, with unsupported selections returning `ASX_E_PERMISSION_DENIED`; remaining debt is actual implementation breadth rather than an unmodeled public contract |
 | `sqlite`, `postgres`, `mysql` unsupported on browser builds | Browser boundary denies the shipped database family in browser mode and backend variants are now explicit compile-time absences | `partial` | `ASX_SURFACE_DATABASE` is fail-closed at the boundary and in the status-returning database entry points, `include/asx/asx_config.h` exposes `ASX_HAS_DATABASE_SURFACE == 0` under browser builds, and the backend-specific variants are now represented explicitly via `ASX_HAS_DB_SQLITE_BACKEND == 0`, `ASX_HAS_DB_POSTGRES_BACKEND == 0`, `ASX_HAS_DB_MYSQL_BACKEND == 0`, and `ASX_HAS_DB_BACKEND_VARIANTS == 0`; remaining debt is backend-specific implementation, not an implicit contract gap |
 | `kafka` unsupported on browser builds | Browser boundary denies the shipped messaging/broker family in browser mode and Kafka is now an explicit compile-time absence | `partial` | `ASX_SURFACE_MESSAGING` is fail-closed at the boundary and in the status-returning messaging entry points, `include/asx/asx_config.h` exposes `ASX_HAS_MESSAGING_SURFACE == 0` under browser builds, and the broker-specific variant is now represented explicitly via `ASX_HAS_MESSAGING_KAFKA_BACKEND == 0` and `ASX_HAS_MESSAGING_BACKEND_VARIANTS == 0`; remaining debt is actual backend-specific broker coverage rather than an undocumented split |
 | Native-only modules (`fs`, `grpc`, `messaging`, `process`, `server`, `signal`) excluded from wasm32 | Browser boundary denies the currently shipped native-facing families | `partial` | The boundary now covers filesystem/process/signal/io/blocking plus explicit `server`/`grpc`/`messaging`/`tls`/`database` surfaces, and CI now runs focused browser-profile suites over shipped browser-safe `actor`/`sync`/`bytes`/`encoding`/`decoding`/`stream`/`security`/`plan`/`cx`/`link`/`app`/`console`/`tracing_compat`/`evidence`/`monitor`/`record`/`migration`/`raptorq`/`spork` plus `net`/`http`/`web`/`websocket`/`quic`/`distributed`/`pipe` and `service`/`transport`/`remote`; remaining debt is compile-time feature gating plus future higher-surface matrix growth |
